methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 collateral tokens escrowed in each LendingTerm (params.collateralToken balances held by term contracts)
CREDIT ERC20 (minted/burned; held by users; held by ProfitManager as surplus buffer; moved through LendingTerm repayments and AuctionHouse bids)
GUILD ERC20 (minted/burned; held by users; also minted/held by SurplusGuildMinter and staked as gauge weight)
Peg token ERC20 in SimplePSM (pegTokenBalance accounting; actual ERC20 balance held by PSM)
Rate-limited minting capacity/buffers in RateLimitedMinter instances (CREDIT and GUILD emission capacity)
Auction proceeds (CREDIT transferred from bidders to LendingTerm; collateral transferred from LendingTerm to bidders/borrowers)
Governance-controlled CREDIT flows: ProfitManager profit splits to surplus buffer, GUILD voters, CREDIT rebasing distribution, and optional otherRecipient transfers
ETH temporarily held/forwarded via CoreRef.emergencyAction (payable multicall sending ETH to arbitrary targets)
state_assets: LendingTerm: loans[loanId] (borrower, times, borrowAmount, collateralAmount, call state, close state), issuance, lastPartialRepay mapping, refs and params structs

ProfitManager: creditMultiplier, surplusBuffer, termSurplusBuffer[term], gaugeProfitIndex[gauge], userGaugeProfitIndex[user][gauge], profitSharingConfig, gaugeWeightTolerance, _minBorrow, references (guild/credit/psm)

GuildToken: gauge voting state (getUserGaugeWeight, getUserWeight, getGaugeWeight, totalWeight, totalTypeWeight, gaugeType, _gauges/_deprecatedGauges/_userGauges), loss state (lastGaugeLoss, lastGaugeLossApplied), transferable flag, profitManager address, multi-votes delegation state
CreditToken: rebasingState[account] shares, totalRebasingShares, interpolated share price and unminted rewards, multi-votes delegation state, ERC20 balances/allowances, EIP-2612 nonces
ERC20MultiVotes: maxDelegates, canContractExceedMaxDelegates allowlist, checkpoints, delegated vote counts, delegate sets, userDelegatedVotes
SurplusGuildMinter: mintRatio, rewardRatio, per-user-per-term stake records (_stakes[user][term]) including credit/guild amounts and indices
LendingTermOnboarding: implementations allowlist, created[term] timestamps, lastProposal[term], immutable references (guildToken, gaugeType, profitManager, auctionHouse, creditMinter, creditToken)
LendingTermOffboarding: polls[snapshotBlock][term] weights, userPollVotes, lastPollBlock, canOffboard, nOffboardingsInProgress, quorum
GuildGovernor/GuildVetoGovernor: proposal/vote state in OZ Governor base contracts, veto timelock mapping (_timelockIds), quorum variables
Core: role admin hierarchy and role membership sets (AccessControlEnumerable)
GuildTimelockController: OZ timelock operation state (timestamps, scheduled ops) but authorization is delegated to Core roles via hasRole override
CoreRef: core pointer (critical reference), paused state, emergencyAction callable
privilege_assets: CoreRoles.GOVERNOR (super-admin across Core, can grant/revoke roles; can setCore() on CoreRef-derived contracts; can call emergencyAction; can change governance parameters; can pause/unpause via GUARDIAN role assignment)
CoreRoles.GUARDIAN (pause/unpause on all CoreRef-derived contracts exposing pause()/unpause())
CoreRoles.CREDIT_MINTER and CoreRoles.GUILD_MINTER (direct mint rights on tokens)
CoreRoles.RATE_LIMITED_CREDIT_MINTER / RATE_LIMITED_GUILD_MINTER (rights to call RateLimitedMinter.mint/replenishBuffer; assigned to LendingTerms and SurplusGuildMinter flows)
CoreRoles.GAUGE_ADD/GAUGE_REMOVE/GAUGE_PARAMETERS (control the gauge set and parameters in GuildToken)
CoreRoles.GAUGE_PNL_NOTIFIER (ability to call ProfitManager.notifyPnL; also required for LendingTerm to start auctions via AuctionHouse.startAuction)
CoreRoles.GUILD_SURPLUS_BUFFER_WITHDRAW (ability to withdraw CREDIT from ProfitManager buffers)
CoreRoles.CREDIT_GOVERNANCE_PARAMETERS / GUILD_GOVERNANCE_PARAMETERS (set maxDelegates / allow exceed lists)
reputation_assets: Gauge voting power and allocations (GUILD weights per gauge drive debt ceilings and profit distribution)
Governance proposal outcomes in GuildGovernor and GuildVetoGovernor (ability to onboard/offboard terms; ability to cancel timelock operations)
Staked positions in SurplusGuildMinter (CREDIT staked as first-loss; minted GUILD voting power attributed via gauge weight)
CREDIT holder rebasing participation state (rebasing opt-in affects receipt of creditSplit rewards)
Protocol solvency perception via ProfitManager.creditMultiplier and surplusBuffer levels (system-wide haircut indicator)
Timelock operation queue integrity and veto process state (timelock timestamps, veto proposals mapping)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: Core is AccessControlEnumerable-based central ACL. CoreRef (inherits OZ Pausable) is embedded by most protocol contracts (LendingTerm, ProfitManager, SimplePSM, AuctionHouse, RateLimitedV2/RateLimitedMinter, governors, SurplusGuildMinter, timelock wrapper) and exposes pause/unpause/setCore/emergencyAction with Core role checks. Tokens: CreditToken inherits CoreRef + ERC20Burnable + ERC20MultiVotes + ERC20RebaseDistributor (which itself inherits OZ ERC20). GuildToken inherits CoreRef + ERC20Burnable + ERC20Gauges + ERC20MultiVotes (both inherit OZ ERC20/Permit) and overrides key gauge weight mutation hooks with ProfitManager + LendingTerm checks. Governance: GuildGovernor is OZ Governor stack (GovernorVotes, GovernorTimelockControl, GovernorSettings, GovernorCountingSimple) plus CoreRef gating for settings + guardianCancel. LendingTermOnboarding extends GuildGovernor and restricts proposing to term-onboarding only; also acts as a Clones factory for LendingTerm implementations. GuildVetoGovernor is a specialized OZ Governor variant (Governor + GovernorVotes) with custom state() to veto/cancel TimelockController operations. Offboarding is a separate CoreRef poll contract (LendingTermOffboarding) interacting with GuildToken and SimplePSM. AuctionHouse is a CoreRef auction executor calling LendingTerm.onBid callback.
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin: ERC20, ERC20Permit, ERC20Burnable, SafeERC20, IERC20/IERC20Permit, Pausable, AccessControl/AccessControlEnumerable, EnumerableSet, Governor framework (Governor, GovernorVotes, GovernorTimelockControl, GovernorSettings, GovernorCountingSimple), TimelockController, Clones, Strings, Math
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No proxy standard (UUPS/Transparent/Beacon/Diamond) is present in the provided code. However, LendingTerm is intended to be used behind minimal proxies (OpenZeppelin Clones). The LendingTerm implementation constructor sets core to address(1) to deter initialization on the implementation, while each clone must call initialize() once. No upgrade hook exists for clones beyond governance creating new terms and migrating.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Centralized role-based access control via Core (AccessControlEnumerable). CoreRef.onlyCoreRole(role) gates privileged functions in all CoreRef-derived contracts, including pausing (GUARDIAN), parameter setters (GOVERNOR or parameter roles), minting roles, gauge management roles, and timelock roles. GuildTimelockController bypasses OZ TimelockController role storage and defers hasRole checks to Core. Some critical public functions are intentionally permissionless (borrowing/repayments, auctions bidding, gauge weight operations, offboarding polling, donating to buffers, distribute/enterRebase/exitRebase).

entry_points.function: initialize(address,(address profitManager,address guildToken,address auctionHouse,address creditMinter,address creditToken),(address collateralToken,uint256 maxDebtPerCollateralToken,uint256 interestRate,uint256 maxDelayBetweenPartialRepay,uint256 minPartialRepayPercent,uint256 openingFee,uint256 hardCap))

state_changes: ERC20 totalSupply (mint to realize rewards, if any)
ERC20 totalSupply (mint to realize rewards, if any)
delegation state of msg.sender (may undelegate previous then re-delegate all free votes)
GuildToken gauge weights for term (incrementGauge via this contract's GUILD balance/weight)
_stakes[user][term] (profitIndex updates; may zero stake on slash)

entry_points.function: createTerm(address,(address collateralToken,uint256 maxDebtPerCollateralToken,uint256 interestRate,uint256 maxDelayBetweenPartialRepay,uint256 minPartialRepayPercent,uint256 openingFee,uint256 hardCap))

state_changes: gauge/user weights for who (decrement full weight on gauge)
ERC20MultiVotes delegation state (via _burn override freeing votes)
ERC20Gauges user gauge sets (may remove gauge)
gauge weights may be decremented to free tokens
delegations may be decremented to free tokens
external_calls: ProfitManager(profitManager).claimGaugeRewards(user, gauge) (indirectly via overridden _decrementGaugeWeight during freeing algorithm)

state_changes: gauge weights may be decremented to free tokens
delegations may be decremented to free tokens
peg token balance of PSM (transfer out)
rebasing share accounting (if to is rebasing)
none in this contract; arbitrary changes in target contracts

upgradeability_surface.notes: Used as EIP-1167 clone implementation via LendingTermOnboarding.createTerm(); not a proxy upgrade pattern. Initialization is permissionless on a fresh clone; factory immediately initializes, but a race exists if a clone is created elsewhere or initialization is not performed atomically by the deployer of the clone.
Factory uses OpenZeppelin Clones to deploy minimal proxies of LendingTerm implementations.
Wraps OZ TimelockController; authorization bypasses TimelockController's own AccessControl storage and uses Core roles.
